mission.title: Install and Configure ClickHouse
mission.description: ClickHouse is a real-time analytics database management system designed for generating analytical data reports quickly. It is ideal for applications that require high-performance data processing and real-time analytics. Key trade-offs include the need for careful schema design to optimize performance and potential complexity in managing distributed setups.

steps.title: Add the ClickHouse Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official ClickHouse Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add clickhouse https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse

```

> This URL points to the official ClickHouse Helm chart repository.

steps.title: Install ClickHouse CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions required for ClickHouse to function properly in Kubernetes.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/master/helm/clickhouse/templates/crds.yaml

```

> This command applies the CRDs from the ClickHouse repository.

steps.title: Install ClickHouse using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs ClickHouse in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install clickhouse clickhouse/clickhouse --namespace clickhouse --create-namespace --version 26.2.4.23-stable

```

> This installs ClickHouse with the specified version in a new namespace.

steps.title: Create a ClickHouse table
steps.description:
This step creates a sample table in ClickHouse to verify the installation.

```bash

kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pods --namespace clickhouse -l app=clickhouse -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -- clickhouse-client --query='CREATE TABLE test (id UInt32, name String) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id;'

```

> This command creates a simple table in ClickHouse.
commands: kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pods --namespace clickhouse -l app=clickhouse -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -- clickhouse-client --query='CREATE TABLE test (id UInt32, name String) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id;'

steps.title: Verify ClickHouse installation
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the ClickHouse pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods --namespace clickhouse

```

> You should see the ClickHouse pods in a Running state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall ClickHouse
uninstall.description:
This step removes ClickHouse and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall clickhouse --namespace clickhouse

kubectl delete namespace clickhouse

```

> This command uninstalls ClickHouse from the specified namespace.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade ClickHouse
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step will upgrade ClickHouse to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pods --namespace clickhouse -l app=clickhouse -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -- clickhouse-client --query='BACKUP TABLE test TO 'backup_location';'

helm upgrade clickhouse clickhouse/clickhouse --namespace clickhouse --version 26.2.4.23-stable

kubectl get pods --namespace clickhouse

```

> Backup your data before proceeding.
> This command upgrades ClickHouse to the specified version.
> Verify that the ClickHouse pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pods --namespace clickhouse -l app=clickhouse -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -- clickhouse-client --query='BACKUP TABLE test TO 'backup_location';'

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cannot connect to ClickHouse server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The ClickHouse server may not be running or is unreachable.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the ClickHouse pods using 'kubectl get pods --namespace clickhouse' and ensure they are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Table test doesn't exist'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified table may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Re-run the table creation command and check for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The ClickHouse pod may not have enough resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits in the ClickHouse Helm chart values and redeploy.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create database'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The ClickHouse server may not have the necessary permissions.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the ClickHouse user has the correct permissions to create databases.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new analytical functions and improved query performance.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v25.x, ensure to migrate your data using the new backup commands.

resolution.summary: You should see the ClickHouse pods in a Running state when executing 'kubectl get pods --namespace clickhouse'.
codeSnippets: kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pods --namespace clickhouse -l app=clickhouse -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -- clickhouse-client --query='CREATE TABLE test (id UInt32, name String) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id;'

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
